title: Colorectal Cancer Screening Tests: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
ome 
Disadvantages
Can have false positives 
Cannot detect precancerous changes 
May miss some cancers 
Colonoscopy will be needed if results are abnormal 
Should be done every three years 
References 
American Cancer Society [Internet]. Atlanta: American Cancer Society Inc.; c2024. American Cancer Society Guideline for Colorectal Cancer Screening; [updated 2024 Jan 29; cited 2024 Apr 27]; [about 4 screens]. Available from: https://www.cancer.org/cancer/colon-rectal-cancer/detection-diagnosis-staging/acs-recommendations.html 
American Cancer Society [Internet]. Atlanta: American Cancer Society Inc.; c2024. Can Colorectal Polyps and Cancer Be Found Early?; [updated 2024 Jan 29; cited 2024 Apr 27]; [about 2 screens]. Available from: https://www.cancer.org/cancer/colon-rectal-cancer/detection-diagnosis-staging/detection.html 
American Cancer Society [Internet]. Atlanta: American Cancer Society Inc.; c2024. Colorectal Cancer Screening Tests; [updated 2024 Mar 04; cited 2024 Apr 27]; [about 5 screens]. Available from: https://www.cancer.org/cancer/colon-rectal-cancer/detection-diagnosis-staging/screening-tests-used.html 
Amitay EL, Gies A, Weigl K, Brenner H. Fecal Immunochemical Tests for Colorectal Cancer Screening: Is Fecal Sampling from Multiple Sites Necessary? Cancers (Basel). 2019 Mar 21; cited 2024 May 02;11(3):400. doi: 10.3390/cancers11030400. PMID: 30901946; PMCID: PMC646
